relations: Astronauts live and work on the space station, conducting daily tasks and experiments
Mission Control provides astronauts with daily instructions, coordinates their activities, and addresses any issues that arise
Astronauts conduct scientific experiments as part of their daily tasks on the space station
Astronauts perform maintenance work on the space station's systems and equipment as part of their daily tasks
Astronauts marvel at the beauty of Earth from space, highlighting a unique relationship between them and the planet
Scientific experiments are conducted on the space station to take advantage of the microgravity conditions
Maintenance work is essential for the smooth operation and safety of the space station
Astronauts perform their duties and live on the space station
The space station orbits Earth, allowing astronauts to view it from space
Mission Control oversees the operations and well-being of the space station and its crew
Astronauts have a final check-in with Mission Control before going to sleep
Astronauts exercise to counteract the effects of microgravity
Astronauts use treadmills as part of their daily exercise regimen
Astronauts use resistance machines alongside treadmills for exercise
